isolation.inspectionMode: Read-only inspection of checked-in mini/ release-preflight source plus shared dry-run evidence; no write-heavy mini build/validate reruns, no browser automation, and no live WeChat upload/runtime claims.
constraints: Did not rerun write-heavy mini build, validate, or upload flows.
Used shared dry-run and validator evidence prepared by the parent workflow.
No browser automation, no WeChat runtime claims, and no live upload claims were made.

commandsRun: Read/Grep/LS inspection of mission guidance, validation contract, mini release-preflight source files, and shared dry-run artifacts.

python3: copied shared npm-upload-dry-run.txt, npm-upload-dry-run-ready.txt, npm-validate.txt, validation-summary.json, manual-upload-handoff.json, manual-upload-handoff.md, and shared-evidence-index.json into mission evidence/mini-upload-shell/wechat-ci-secret-gate/ and wrote git-ls-files-local-only.txt.

assertions.title: The miniprogram-ci path is scaffolded but hard-gated behind injected secrets
assertions.proofSummary: The checked-in mini/ release surface preserves a later-activatable miniprogram-ci upload path, but the scaffold fails closed until operator-injected key material and metadata are provided, dry-run evidence reaches a ready/no-upload posture without printing the injected secret, and local-only secret paths remain outside versioned source.

sourceReferences.note: Checked-in scripts expose preflight and upload:wechat, and the manifest declares miniprogram-ci as a dependency for the later activation path.
The lockfile preserves the miniprogram-ci dependency for clean installs.

Defines the required injected env keys, keeps private keys local-only, rejects keys placed inside versioned source, refuses missing inputs or non-dry-run uploads without explicit enablement, and lazily loads miniprogram-ci only after the gate passes.

Regression tests cover clean-install dependency wiring, missing-input refusal, dry-run readiness without secret echo, refusal of versioned-source private-key paths, the live-upload enable gate, and the later activation path once injected inputs exist.

Offline preflight requires the upload scaffold script to stay exposed, verifies gitignore coverage for local-only paths, inspects the git index for tracked/staged secret artifacts, and keeps the handoff honest.
Checked-in handoff copy states that the miniprogram-ci scaffold requires operator-injected secrets and that local-only secret paths must remain untracked.
The shared runtime config defines the manual-upload truth boundary and the expected local-only override/upload-secret paths.
The repo ignores mini/project.private.config.json, mini/config/runtime.local.js, mini/config/runtime.local.json, and mini/upload-secrets/.

artifactReferences.note: Shared dry-run evidence refuses to proceed without injected secret material plus robot/version/description metadata and explicitly says no private key belongs in versioned source.
Shared dry-run-ready evidence shows the gate passes with simulated injected inputs, remains no-upload evidence only, and reports only env:WECHAT_MINI_CI_PRIVATE_KEY instead of key contents.
Shared validator output passes the miniprogram-ci clean-install, missing-secret refusal, secret non-echo, versioned-source rejection, live-upload gate, and later activation tests.
Generated summary records the local-only upload-secret paths and repeats that the checked-in scaffold refuses to proceed until operator-injected secrets and upload metadata are supplied.
Checked-in handoff material keeps the truth boundary honest and lists the local-only upload-secret paths and deferred operator steps.
Local git index proof shows no tracked or staged Mini private-config or upload-secret paths.

proofChecks.name: checked-in miniprogram-ci path exists for later activation
proofChecks.summary: The checked-in mini manifest exposes upload:wechat and depends on miniprogram-ci, the lockfile preserves it for clean installs, the upload helper lazily loads the dependency, and the validator suite covers the later activation path once injected inputs exist.

proofChecks.name: scaffold refuses to proceed without injected secrets and metadata
proofChecks.summary: The upload helper requires injected private-key material, robot slot, version, and description, and the shared refusal log shows the scaffold stops with actionable guidance when those values are absent.

proofChecks.name: dry run reaches ready/no-upload posture without echoing secret material
proofChecks.summary: With simulated injected inputs the shared dry-run log reaches DRY RUN READY, states that no upload was attempted, and prints only the env key name; the validator suite separately asserts the inline secret value is never echoed.

proofChecks.name: private key material stays outside versioned source and the scaffold expects injected local secrets
proofChecks.summary: Source, handoff copy, gitignore rules, and git index proof all keep runtime-local config and upload keys outside tracked source, while the upload helper explicitly rejects private-key paths placed inside versioned source.

generatedEvidence.derivedFrom: git ls-files --cached against mini/project.private.config.json, mini/config/runtime.local.js, mini/upload-secrets, and mini/upload-secrets/code-upload.private.key

summary: Tested the assigned release-preflight assertion VAL-RELEASE-002: passed. Evidence stays limited to checked-in miniprogram-ci secret-gate and dry-run readiness proof; no real WeChat upload, runtime, device, or publish success is claimed.
